author: Jane Austen
description: A classic novel about love, society, and personal growth.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel about surveillance, control, and totalitarianism.

name: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story of justice, childhood, and moral courage in the American South.

name: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A novel of wealth, longing, and illusion in the glittering Jazz Age.

name: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A fantasy adventure following Bilbo Baggins on an unexpected journey.

name: The Catcher in the Rye

description: A coming-of-age novel exploring alienation, identity, and youth.

name: Brave New World

description: A futuristic novel about technology, control, and the cost of comfort.

name: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Bronte
description: A deeply emotional novel about independence, love, and resilience.

description: A moving story about words, loss, and humanity during World War II.

name: Little Women

description: A beloved novel about family, ambition, and the lives of four sisters.

name: Sense and Sensibility

description: A romance of two sisters balancing reason, emotion, and social expectation.

name: Persuasion
author: Jane Austen
description: A mature love story about second chances, regret, and constancy.

name: Homage to Catalonia
author: George Orwell
description: A politically sharp narrative about war, ideology, and disillusionment.

name: Go Set a Watchman

description: A reflective classic revisiting familiar characters and difficult moral questions.

name: The Fellowship of the Ring

description: The opening epic of a perilous journey across Middle-earth.

name: Shirley
